series.title: Envoy AI Gateway v0.6.x
series.subtitle: v1beta1 API graduation, AWS Bedrock InvokeModel for Claude, Anthropic endpoint on OpenAI backends, Gemini embeddings and context caching, MCP per-backend header forwarding, GKE Workload Identity, and request/response body redaction

releases.overview: Envoy AI Gateway v0.6.0 graduates the core CRDs (AIGatewayRoute, AIServiceBackend, BackendSecurityPolicy, GatewayConfig, MCPRoute) to v1beta1, signaling production-readiness of the API surface. AWS Bedrock gains native InvokeModel support for Claude alongside cross-provider translation between OpenAI and Anthropic schemas. Gemini gets first-class embeddings and prefix-based context caching. MCP gains per-backend header forwarding with rename and JWT claim propagation. Operators get GKE Workload Identity, configurable webhook host networking, sensitive data redaction, and faster Go 1.26 builds.

items.title: Native InvokeModel API for Claude
items.description: Send requests to Claude models on Bedrock through Bedrock's native InvokeModel endpoint, complementing the existing Converse API path. Useful when applications already speak the Anthropic Messages format and want a thin translation layer.

items.title: OpenAI → Bedrock embeddings translation
items.description: Call Amazon Titan and Cohere embedding models on Bedrock through the standard OpenAI /v1/embeddings contract. Switch embedding providers without changing client code.

items.title: Bedrock Titan embeddings routing
items.description: Dataplane routing for Titan embeddings models is now wired up by default, so Titan endpoints work out of the box.

items.title: Anthropic /v1/messages endpoint on OpenAI backends
items.description: Expose any OpenAI-compatible backend through Anthropic's Messages API. Lets Claude-style clients reach OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, or any other OpenAI-compatible provider behind the gateway without rewriting requests.

items.title: Structured output for Claude models
items.description: Pass JSON schema constraints through to Claude so responses conform to your declared shape. Applies across Anthropic, AWS Bedrock, and GCP Vertex AI Claude backends.

items.title: Default max_tokens for Anthropic translator
items.description: Anthropic requests without an explicit max_tokens now get a sensible default instead of failing at the provider, smoothing over a common footgun when forwarding OpenAI-shaped requests.

items.title: Adaptive thinking for claude-opus-4.6
items.description: Translate the new adaptive thinking mode end-to-end so callers can opt into Claude's latest reasoning controls without bespoke provider code.

items.title: Reasoning effort mapping for Claude
items.description: Map OpenAI's reasoning_effort field onto Claude's thinking budgets via the Anthropic API, giving you a single knob across providers.

items.title: Gemini embeddings translation
items.description: Use Gemini embedding models through the OpenAI /v1/embeddings contract, completing Gemini coverage alongside chat completions and Responses.

items.title: Gemini context caching with prefix-style API
items.description: Activate Gemini's context caching using the same Anthropic-style prefix caching surface already supported elsewhere. Cut input token costs on long, repeated system prompts without a Gemini-specific code path.

items.title: Reasoning effort mapping for gemini-3-flash
items.description: reasoning_effort now maps to Gemini 3's thinking controls, so the same client knob works across Anthropic, OpenAI, and Gemini.

items.title: Gemini reasoning surfaced as thinking blocks
items.description: Non-streaming Gemini reasoning is now exposed as both string content and structured thinking_blocks, matching the shape clients already use for Anthropic responses.

items.title: Open Responses API compatibility
items.description: Improved compatibility with the open ecosystem variant of the Responses API, broadening which Responses-aware clients can sit in front of the gateway.

items.title: Responses API — phase 2
items.description: Second wave of Responses API work fills in features such as context management and richer streaming so the /v1/responses path is closer to parity with chat completions.

items.title: Text-to-speech endpoint /v1/audio/speech
items.description: Route OpenAI text-to-speech requests through the gateway, so audio workloads benefit from the same auth, rate limiting, and observability as chat traffic.

items.title: Batch inference APIs
items.description: Forward OpenAI batch inference endpoints, enabling cheaper async workloads to flow through the same gateway as interactive traffic.

items.title: Per-backend header forwarding with rename
items.description: MCPRouteBackendRef now accepts forwardHeaders entries, including renaming, so each MCP backend can receive its own set of inbound request headers (e.g., trace context or tenant identifiers).

items.title: JWT claim forwarding to MCP backends
items.description: Project verified JWT claims into headers the gateway forwards to backend MCP servers, enabling identity-aware tool execution without re-authenticating downstream.

items.title: Exclude / excludeRegex on tool selectors
items.description: Hide tools from clients with explicit deny patterns alongside the existing include rules, useful when a backend exposes more capabilities than a given route should surface.

items.title: Tool name in access logs and response metadata
items.description: Tool invocations now show up in access logs and response metadata, making per-tool debugging and analytics straightforward.

items.title: Per-backend capability tracking
items.description: The gateway tracks which capabilities each backend supports, so capability negotiation reflects what's actually reachable from a given route.

items.title: GKE Workload Identity via Application Default Credentials
items.description: GCP backends now authenticate using the standard ADC chain, so workloads running on GKE pick up Workload Identity automatically — no static service account keys needed.

items.title: Hardened bearer token parsing
items.description: Malformed Authorization headers no longer panic the extproc; they now fall through to the standard auth failure path.

items.title: Request and response body redaction
items.description: Strip or mask sensitive fields in request and response bodies before they hit logs, traces, or metrics. Lets you keep observability on while meeting privacy and compliance constraints.

items.title: OTLP access logging auto-configured by aigw
items.description: Standalone aigw wires up OTLP access logging out of the box when an OTLP endpoint is configured, removing a manual step from the local-dev and demo paths.

items.title: ReasoningToken cost type
items.description: LLMRequestCostType now includes ReasoningToken, so you can budget and bill against thinking tokens separately from input and output.

items.title: Response model metadata
items.description: Responses now carry the resolved upstream model in metadata, which clients and downstream tools can read to confirm exactly which model served a request.

items.title: OTEL attribute count cap removed for large contexts
items.description: Removed the OTEL span attribute count limit so long-context requests no longer have parts of their trace silently dropped.

items.title: Custom webhook port and host network
items.description: The conversion webhook can now bind to a configurable port and run on the host network, smoothing installs in clusters with restrictive admission webhook networking (e.g., GKE private clusters).

items.title: Lua filter in afterExtProcFilterPrefixes
items.description: Insert Lua filters after the ExtProc stage in the standard filter chain, useful for last-mile request shaping without writing a custom EnvoyExtensionPolicy.

items.title: Route-scoped LLM request costs with global defaults
items.description: Costs can be set per-route, with global defaults applied otherwise. Makes per-tenant or per-backend cost tracking straightforward without per-route boilerplate.

apiChanges.title: Core CRDs promoted to aigateway.envoyproxy.io/v1beta1
apiChanges.description: AIGatewayRoute, AIServiceBackend, BackendSecurityPolicy, and GatewayConfig are now served at v1beta1. v1alpha1 is still served as a conversion target during the upgrade window.

apiChanges.title: MCPRoute promoted to v1beta1
apiChanges.description: MCPRoute moves to v1beta1, signaling that the MCP routing API is stable enough for production use.

apiChanges.title: MCPRouteBackendRef.forwardHeaders
apiChanges.description: New per-backend list of headers to forward, with optional rename. Replaces the need for a single route-wide header forwarding rule when backends expect different headers.

apiChanges.title: MCPRouteSecurityPolicy JWT claim forwarding
apiChanges.description: Configure which verified JWT claims should be projected into outbound headers to MCP backends.

apiChanges.title: MCPToolSelector exclude / excludeRegex
apiChanges.description: Tool selectors now support exclusion alongside inclusion, with both literal and regex forms.

apiChanges.title: LLMRequestCostType.ReasoningToken
apiChanges.description: New cost type for thinking-token usage, complementing the existing input, output, and cache cost types.

apiChanges.title: Backend quota policy API
apiChanges.description: New API surface for declaring upstream-provider quota policies, laying the groundwork for quota-aware routing.

breakingChanges.title: AIGatewayRoute.spec.filterConfig removed
breakingChanges.description: The filterConfig field on AIGatewayRoute has been removed. Move external-processor configuration (resources, env vars, image overrides) to a GatewayConfig resource referenced from the Gateway via the aigateway.envoyproxy.io/gateway-config annotation. Note: v0.5 shipped without an explicit deprecation warning for this field, so users still relying on it must migrate as part of the v0.6 upgrade.

bugFixes.title: Webhook cache race during extProc injection
bugFixes.description: The conversion webhook now uses a non-cached reader to avoid a race where stale cache reads could cause extProc injection to misfire on freshly applied resources.

bugFixes.title: Field ownership preserved on updates
bugFixes.description: Controller updates no longer claim ownership of fields they shouldn't, preventing churn and conflicts when other controllers or operators co-manage adjacent fields.

bugFixes.title: Orphan cleanup for MCPRoute backendrefs
bugFixes.description: Resources tied to MCPRoute backend references are now cleaned up when the route or reference is removed, fixing a leak that could leave stale config in the cluster.

bugFixes.title: Standalone Envoy startup failures surfaced by CLI
bugFixes.description: aigw now reports standalone Envoy startup failures cleanly instead of hanging or printing an unhelpful trace, making local dev and CI loops much faster to diagnose.

bugFixes.title: Bedrock Titan embeddings dataplane route
bugFixes.description: Restored the Envoy route for Titan embeddings in dataplane tests so Titan workloads exercise the full pipeline.

bugFixes.title: Bearer token parsing panic
bugFixes.description: Malformed bearer tokens in the Authorization header used to panic the subject extractor; they now return a clean auth failure.

bugFixes.title: Request context propagation in PostTranslateModify
bugFixes.description: The request context now flows into Kubernetes client calls made from PostTranslateModify, so cancellation and deadlines work as expected.

bugFixes.title: Case-sensitive JSON marshalling and unmarshalling
bugFixes.description: JSON encoding now consistently honors case, fixing subtle mismatches when round-tripping fields whose names differ only in case.

dependencies.title: Go 1.26.2
dependencies.description: Updated to Go 1.26.2 to pick up the latest security and performance fixes.

dependencies.title: Envoy Gateway v1.7.0
dependencies.description: Built on Envoy Gateway v1.7.0 for the newest data plane capabilities and stability fixes.

dependencies.title: Envoy v1.37
dependencies.description: Leveraging Envoy Proxy v1.37.0 for the latest networking and security features.

dependencies.title: Gateway API v1.4.1
dependencies.description: Support for Gateway API v1.4.1 specifications.

dependencies.title: Gateway API Inference Extension v1.0.2
dependencies.description: Continued integration with Gateway API Inference Extension v1.0.2 for stable intelligent endpoint selection.

dependencies.title: MCP Go SDK 1.4.1
dependencies.description: Updated to modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk v1.4.1 for the latest MCP protocol features and fixes.
